description:
Tighten the bounded epic loop so `completed` is only returned when: all tickets are terminal, no workers are active/exited-unverified, and integrated validation + scope review have no unresolved `fix` findings.

## What to change

In `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/orchestrator.ts`, in `runBoundedEpicLoop`:

Currently the loop can return `completed` when all children are closed. This must change:

1. After detecting "all children closed", check for any exited-but-unverified current-branch workers
2. If unverified workers exist, trigger their verification inline by calling `inspectWorkerRuntime` on each (which invokes `verifyCurrentBranchWorker` via the orchestration-lock pattern). This is NOT "waiting" in a background sense — it's synchronous orchestration within the loop iteration.
3. After all workers are verified/landed AND all tickets closed, trigger scope-completion validation (run configured validation commands in the checkout)
4. After scope validation passes, trigger scope-completion review
5. Only return `completed` when validation passes AND scope review has no unresolved `fix` findings
6. If scope-level `fix` findings create new work (new beadwork children), continue the loop with the new tasks

## How "waiting" works

The bounded loop does NOT sleep or poll. When the loop detects unverified workers, it calls `inspectWorkerRuntime` for each. That function already has the orchestration-lock pattern that runs `verifyCurrentBranchWorker` inline and awaits its completion. So the loop naturally "waits" by awaiting the promise from the verification orchestration.

If verification routes a worker to `attention`, the loop breaks with the existing `attention` stop reason rather than `completed`.

## Design Rules

- This is a required behavior change from current code
- The loop must still stop for `attention`/`blocked`/`empty`/`max-cycles` as before
- For standalone `/bw delegate <ticket>`: the scope IS the single ticket, run validation once, NO extra scope review
- Scope-level `fix` iterations capped at 2 rounds; if not converged → `attention`
- Do NOT add a new "batch" or "cycle" abstraction — use existing loop semantics
- Validation commands are the same ones already configured for worktree validation — they just run in the shared checkout instead of a worktree

## Tests

- Loop does NOT return `completed` while workers are exited-but-unverified
- Loop does NOT return `completed` while scope validation is pending
- Loop does NOT return `completed` while scope review has unresolved `fix` findings
- Loop returns `completed` only after full verification pipeline passes
- Standalone delegate uses single-ticket scope (no scope review beyond worker review)
- 2-round cap on scope-level fixes
- Existing `attention`/`blocked`/`empty`/`max-cycles` stops unchanged
- Verification failure (worker → attention) causes loop to stop with `attention`

## Definition of Done

- Bounded loop stop conditions tightened for current-branch mode
- Scope-completion gates enforced
- Worktree mode loop behavior unchanged (worktree workers reach `landed` via existing path)
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean
title: Tighten bounded epic loop stop conditions for scope-completion
